title: Apowollastonia stirlingii

herb_names_common.name: stirling's wolly tree (no english name found)

uses: Apowollastonia stirlingii has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various skin conditions and wounds. Its leaves are often crushed and applied topically to treat sores, cuts, and burns. Additionally, it may be used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. However, more research is needed to fully understand and confirm its medicinal uses.
taxonomy: Apowollastonia stirlingii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae and the genus Apowollastonia. This plant is native to Western Australia, where it grows in well-drained soils. The classification of Apowollastonia stirlingii places it within the larger plant kingdom, specifically in the division Magnoliophyta. Further classification includes the order Asterales and the family Asteraceae, which comprises many other flowering plant species.
